mnemonics: GROUPS: G (Group) R (Ring) O (Operation) U (Unit) P (Properties) S (Set) - Grimaldi's core structure
keyPoints: A group is a set with an operation satisfying closure, associativity, identity, and invertibility
Abelian groups are commutative groups where the operation order doesn't matter
Subgroups are subsets of a group that themselves form a group under the same operation
commonMistakes: Assuming all groups are commutative (only Abelian groups are)
Forgetting to verify closure when proving a subset is a subgroup
Confusing the identity element with the zero element from other algebraic structures
